test_cases.title: Verify User Registration Flow
test_cases.resolution_summary: This test case requires a user registration page that is NOT part of the AURA framework. The AURA framework is designed for HP Instant Ink subscription management and uses HPID (HP ID) for account creation, not a generic registration form. The test requires creating 3 new page objects (HomePage, RegistrationPage, RegistrationSuccessPage) and 1 helper. Only foundation/infrastructure files from AURA can be reused.

relevant_files.reason: Foundation file required for every test - provides test setup, email generation, and common utilities
relevant_files.kb_evidence: KB confirms this is a foundation file required for every test. It is a MODULE (not a class), so class_name is null.

relevant_files.reason: Foundation file required for every test - manages browser lifecycle (launch, close, page creation)
relevant_files.kb_evidence: KB confirms this is a foundation file for browser lifecycle management required in every test.

relevant_files.reason: Foundation file required for every test - provides configuration management, logger, and constants
relevant_files.kb_evidence: KB confirms this is a foundation file for configuration and logging required in every test.

relevant_files.reason: Foundation file required for every test - provides test reporting and validation mechanisms
relevant_files.kb_evidence: KB confirms this file exists at core/allure_reporter.py with class AllureReporter in Community 10.

relevant_files.reason: Base class for all page objects - must be included when any page object is used. Provides generic methods for text entry, waiting, and assertions that will be used by the new RegistrationPage.
relevant_files.kb_evidence: KB confirms BasePageObject is the parent class for all page objects and provides generic methods like enter_text(), wait_for_element_visible(), assert_element_visible().

relevant_files.reason: Foundation file required for every test - provides utility functions including random string generation for unique email addresses
relevant_files.kb_evidence: KB confirms core/utils.py exists in Community 5 and provides utility functions like generate_random_string().

relevant_files.reason: Foundation file required for every test - provides pytest configuration, fixtures (stage_callback, tc_tracer, main_execution, reporter, session_cleanup), and hooks
relevant_files.kb_evidence: KB confirms conftest.py (root level) exists in Community 10 with key fixtures and pytest hooks.

relevant_files.reason: Foundation file required for every test - provides YAML test configuration loading with fixtures (yaml_data, yaml_file_path, testcase_id) and functions (parse_flow, load_yaml_file, expand_imports)
relevant_files.kb_evidence: KB confirms core/conftest.py exists in Community 10 with YAML configuration loading capabilities.

relevant_locators_summary.note: No existing AURA page objects contain locators for this test case. All locators must be created in new page objects.

test_cases.flow_pattern: Navigate to RegistrationPage → Fill multi-field registration form (first name, last name, email, password, confirm password, phone, date of birth) → Submit form → Verify success message on RegistrationSuccessPage

components_to_create.name: HomePage
components_to_create.description: Page object for the application home page with navigation to registration. Must inherit from BasePageObject. Should contain locator for registration_page_link and method to navigate to registration page.
components_to_create.reason: KB search confirms no existing page object handles a generic home page with registration link. The AURA framework starts with LandingPage (Instant Ink specific), not a generic home page.
components_to_create.kb_evidence: KB query for 'home page' or 'registration navigation' returned no matches. LandingPage is Instant Ink specific.

components_to_create.name: RegistrationPage
components_to_create.description: Page object for user registration form. Must inherit from BasePageObject. Should contain locators for: registration_form, first_name_input, last_name_input, email_input, password_input, confirm_password_input, phone_number_input, date_of_birth_input, register_button, and all validation error elements. Should provide methods: enter_first_name(), enter_last_name(), enter_email(), enter_password(), enter_confirm_password(), enter_phone_number(), select_date_of_birth(), click_register_button(), and validation methods. Can use inherited generic methods like enter_text(locator, text) from BasePageObject.
components_to_create.reason: KB search confirms no existing page object handles a generic registration form. HPIDPage handles HPID-specific account creation (firstName, lastName, email, password, create_account_button) but is part of the Instant Ink enrollment flow, not a standalone registration page. The test requires a different registration page with additional fields (phone, date of birth, confirm password) and different flow.
components_to_create.kb_evidence: KB query for 'RegistrationPage' returned no matches. HPIDPage exists but is HPID-specific for Instant Ink enrollment, not a generic registration form.

components_to_create.name: RegistrationSuccessPage
components_to_create.description: Page object for registration success confirmation page. Must inherit from BasePageObject. Should contain locators for: success_message, success_page_heading, success_icon. Should provide methods to verify success message text and page display.
components_to_create.reason: KB search confirms no existing page object handles a generic registration success page. ThankYouPage exists but is for post-enrollment success in the Instant Ink flow, not for standalone registration confirmation.
components_to_create.kb_evidence: KB query for 'RegistrationSuccessPage' or 'success confirmation page' returned no matches. ThankYouPage is enrollment-specific.

components_to_create.name: RegistrationHelper
components_to_create.description: Helper to orchestrate the complete registration flow. Should provide methods: navigate_to_registration_page(page), fill_registration_form(page, user_data), submit_registration(page), verify_registration_success(page, expected_message). Should use the RegistrationPage and RegistrationSuccessPage objects. Can follow the pattern of EnrollmentHelper for multi-step orchestration.
components_to_create.reason: KB search confirms no existing helper orchestrates a generic registration flow. HPIDHelper handles HPID account creation but is specific to the Instant Ink enrollment context. EnrollmentHelper orchestrates enrollment but does not handle standalone registration. A new helper is needed to orchestrate the registration flow for this test.
components_to_create.kb_evidence: KB query for 'registration helper' returned no matches. HPIDHelper and EnrollmentHelper are Instant Ink enrollment-specific.

test_cases.framework_compatibility_notes: This test case is NOT compatible with the AURA framework's domain. AURA is designed for HP Instant Ink subscription management testing, not generic user registration. The framework uses HPID (HP ID) for account creation as part of the enrollment flow, not standalone registration forms. To automate this test case, you would need to create entirely new page objects and helpers that are outside the AURA framework's current scope. Consider whether this test case belongs in a different test suite or if the test case should be rewritten to test HPID account creation within the Instant Ink enrollment context.
kb_queries_performed: What file and class handles the RegistrationPage or user registration page?
What file and class handles the RegistrationSuccessPage or success confirmation page after user registration?
What foundation files are needed for EVERY test in the AURA framework?
What are the ACTUAL methods in test_flows_common.py?
What is the correct file path and import statement for allure_reporter?
What is the correct file path for utils.py?
Does the AURA framework have any page objects or helpers for handling generic registration forms?
What are the actual methods available in BasePageObject?
What helper files exist in the AURA framework?
What helper file handles HPID account creation?
What is the complete list of all page object files in the AURA framework?
test_cases.kb_findings_summary: The AURA framework does not contain page objects or helpers for generic user registration. The framework is domain-specific for HP Instant Ink subscription management. The closest match is HPIDPage (for HPID account creation) but it is part of the enrollment flow, not a standalone registration page. All page objects for this test case must be created from scratch, inheriting from BasePageObject and using its generic methods (enter_text, wait_for_element_visible, assert_element_visible). Only foundation/infrastructure files can be reused.

summary.framework_coverage_assessment: The AURA framework provides only foundational infrastructure (browser management, configuration, base classes, utilities) for this test case. The test case requires domain-specific components (registration pages and helper) that do not exist in the framework because AURA is designed for HP Instant Ink subscription testing, not generic user registration. Framework coverage is 0% for domain-specific components and 100% for infrastructure components.
recommendations: Evaluate whether this test case belongs in the AURA framework or should be part of a different test suite for generic user management
If this test case must be automated in AURA, create the 3 new page objects (HomePage, RegistrationPage, RegistrationSuccessPage) inheriting from BasePageObject
Create a RegistrationHelper following the pattern of EnrollmentHelper to orchestrate the multi-step registration flow
Use core/utils.py generate_random_string() to create unique email addresses for each test run
Follow AURA naming conventions: snake_case for methods/locators, PascalCase for classes
Consider whether the test case should be rewritten to test HPID account creation within the Instant Ink enrollment context instead of generic registration
